---
title: "Hitha Sunil"
subtitle: "Dissemination Ops, iSRL"
description: "Working on dissemination workflows, communication systems, and structured information delivery."
order: 4
team-role: "Dissemination Ops"
team-track: "Core Team"

image: images/hsunilofficial.jpg

about:
  template: trestles
  image: images/hsunilofficial.jpg
  image-shape: round
  links:
    - icon: envelope
      text: hsunilofficial@gmail.com
      href: "mailto:hsunilofficial@gmail.com"
    - icon: github
      text: hsunilofficial
      href: https://github.com/hsunilofficial
    - icon: linkedin
      text: hsunilofficial
      href: https://linkedin.com/in/hsunilofficial
---

```{=html}
<script type="application/ld+json">
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "Person",
  "name": "Hitha Sunil",
  "jobTitle": "Dissemination Ops",
  "description": "Working on dissemination workflows, communication systems, and structured information delivery at iSRL.",
  "url": "https://isrl-research.github.io/team/hsunilofficial.html",
  "email": "hsunilofficial@gmail.com",
  "identifier": "https://orcid.org/0009-0003-3747-7910",
  "sameAs": [
    "https://orcid.org/0009-0003-3747-7910",
    "https://github.com/hsunilofficial",
    "https://linkedin.com/in/hsunilofficial"
  ],
  "worksFor": {
    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "iSRL",
    "url": "https://isrl-research.github.io"
  },
  "affiliation": {
    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "IIT Madras"
  }
}
</script>
```
Dissemination Ops at iSRL — I work on making research actually move.

Not just documenting it, but building the systems, workflows, and structures that take work from “done” to something usable, accessible, and out in the world.

A lot of my work happens in the messy middle — figuring things out when there’s no clear path. Whether it’s picking up unfamiliar tools like Quarto, testing until something works, or knowing when to push through versus when to ask, I focus on making things function reliably, not just theoretically.

My work is less about describing dissemination and more about making it happen — turning complex research into structured outputs that people can understand, navigate, and build on.

I’m also increasingly interested in research itself — especially how ideas are developed, structured, and translated into meaningful outputs. Through my MSc and BSc work, I’ve engaged with research problems while consistently picking up new tools and techniques independently through exploration.

Alongside this, I lead the Localization Engineering department at Sahityika (IIT Madras’ literary society), leading initiatives and exploring integration approaches to make digital content accessible across languages. I care deeply about breaking language barriers and ensuring information reaches people beyond a single linguistic or technical context.

Currently pursuing a second bachelor’s degree in Data Science and Applications at IIT Madras, with plans to transition to Management and Data Science to better align with systems, operations, and research-focused work.

I’m driven by curiosity — especially around how systems work across biology, technology, and space, and how they scale without losing meaning.

I think in systems: breaking processes down, improving consistency, and building structures that don’t just work once, but keep working over time.

Outside work, I focus on structured routines — walking, fitness, and habit systems — exploring how small, consistent actions compound into long-term outcomes.
